games.title: Elden Ring
games.description: Open-world action RPG set in the Lands Between, featuring a world co-created by Hidetaka Miyazaki and George R.R. Martin. Players explore vast landscapes, battle powerful enemies, and uncover the mystery of the shattered Elden Ring.
awards: Game of the Year 2022 - The Game Awards

games.title: God of War: Ragnarök
games.description: Kratos and Atreus embark on a mythic journey through the Nine Realms to prevent Ragnarök. The sequel to God of War (2018) expands the Norse mythology and deepens the father-son relationship at the story's core.
awards: Game of the Year 2022 - DICE Awards

games.title: The Last of Us Part II
games.description: Five years after their dangerous journey across the post-pandemic United States, Ellie and Joel have settled in Jackson. When a violent event disrupts that peace, Ellie embarks on a relentless journey to carry out justice.
awards: Game of the Year 2020 - The Game Awards

games.title: Cyberpunk 2077
games.description: Open-world action RPG set in Night City, a megalopolis obsessed with power, glamour, and body modification. Players take on the role of V, a mercenary outlaw going after a one-of-a-kind implant that is the key to immortality.

games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
games.description: Geralt of Rivia, a monster hunter for hire, searches for his adopted daughter Ciri who is wanted by the otherworldly Wild Hunt. Set in a vast open world with deep narrative choices and consequences.
awards: Game of the Year 2015 - The Game Awards

games.title: Grand Theft Auto VI
games.description: Set in the fictional state of Leonida including Vice City, GTA VI features Lucia as the series' first female protagonist alongside Jason. The game returns to the neon-soaked streets of the American South.

games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
games.description: Link awakens from a 100-year slumber and sets out to defeat the Calamity Ganon in an open-world Hyrule. Pioneered open-world design for Nintendo with dynamic physics and chemistry systems.
awards: Game of the Year 2017 - The Game Awards

games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
games.description: Sequel to Breath of the Wild. Link explores the skies above, the surface of, and the depths below Hyrule, featuring revolutionary Ultrahand and Fuse building mechanics.
awards: Game of the Year 2023 - The Game Awards

games.title: Red Dead Redemption 2
games.description: Set in 1899, outlaw Arthur Morgan navigates the decline of the Wild West while riding with the Van der Linde gang. A prequel to the original Red Dead Redemption, praised for its storytelling and world detail.
awards: Game of the Year 2018 - Golden Joystick Awards

games.title: Marvel's Spider-Man 2
games.description: Peter Parker and Miles Morales face new threats including Kraven the Hunter and the alien symbiote Venom. Players can switch between the two Spider-Men across an expanded New York City.

games.title: Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice
games.description: Set in late 1500s Sengoku Japan, a shinobi named Wolf fights to rescue his lord and seek revenge. Features a posture-based combat system emphasizing deflection over stamina management.
awards: Game of the Year 2019 - The Game Awards

games.title: Baldur's Gate 3
games.description: A Dungeons & Dragons RPG where players must work together to survive a world infected by a parasite of unspeakable power. Set in the Forgotten Realms, featuring deep role-playing and tactical combat.
awards: Game of the Year 2023 - The Game Awards

games.title: Starfield
games.description: Bethesda's first new IP in 25 years — a space exploration RPG set across 1,000 planets in the year 2330. Players join Constellation, an organization seeking mysterious artifacts.

games.title: Horizon Forbidden West
games.description: Aloy ventures west into uncharted terrain to discover the source of a blight threatening both machines and people. Sequel to Horizon Zero Dawn with expanded underwater exploration and new machine types.

games.title: Portal 2
games.description: Chell re-awakens in the Aperture Science Enrichment Center and must work with the bumbling robot Wheatley to escape while facing a resurrected GLaDOS. Widely considered one of the best puzzle games ever made.

games.title: Super Mario Odyssey
games.description: Mario travels across various kingdoms with his hat companion Cappy to rescue Princess Peach from Bowser. Features the Capture mechanic allowing Mario to inhabit enemies and objects.

games.title: Hades
games.description: Zagreus, son of Hades, attempts to escape the Underworld with help from the Olympian gods. Each escape attempt deepens the narrative and unlocks new abilities and story revelations.

games.title: Assassin's Creed Shadows
games.description: Set in feudal Japan, players alternate between Naoe, a skilled shinobi assassin, and Yasuke, a legendary African samurai who served under Nobunaga Oda. Features dynamic seasons and authentic Japanese settings.

games.title: Ghost of Tsushima
games.description: Samurai Jin Sakai must become the Ghost — abandon the way of the samurai — to liberate Tsushima Island from a brutal Mongol invasion in 1274. Features a Kurosawa mode for classic black-and-white presentation.

games.title: Hollow Knight
games.description: A knight explores the vast underground kingdom of Hallownest, battling bugs and discovering ancient secrets. Known for challenging combat, atmospheric world-building, and hours of hidden content.

games.title: Dark Souls III
games.description: The Age of Fire is fading. As an Unkindled warrior, players hunt the Lords of Cinder who abandoned their duty to link the flame. The third entry in the Dark Souls series featuring faster, more aggressive combat.

games.title: Mario Kart 8 Deluxe
games.description: Enhanced port of Mario Kart 8 with all DLC included plus new battle modes. Features 96 tracks including 48 from the Booster Course Pass DLC. Best-selling Nintendo Switch game of all time.

companies.name: Nintendo
current_projects: The Legend of Zelda series continuation
companies.description: Japanese multinational video game company founded in 1889 as a playing card manufacturer. Creator of the Nintendo Switch, Game Boy, NES, and SNES. Known for family-friendly first-party titles and innovative hardware.

companies.name: Santa Monica Studio
companies.description: Sony first-party studio best known for the God of War franchise. Founded in 1999 and based in Los Angeles. Revived the God of War series in 2018 with a Norse mythology setting.

companies.name: Naughty Dog
current_projects: The Last of Us multiplayer standalone
companies.description: Sony first-party studio founded in 1984, originally as JAM Software. Known for technically groundbreaking PlayStation exclusives. Created Crash Bandicoot, Jak and Daxter, Uncharted, and The Last of Us.

companies.name: CD Projekt Red
current_projects: The Witcher 4 (codename: Polaris) - in active development
companies.description: Polish video game developer and publisher founded in 1994. Created The Witcher franchise and Cyberpunk 2077. Known for expansive RPGs with strong narrative focus and free post-launch content updates.

companies.name: Rockstar Games
companies.description: American video game developer and publisher founded in 1998 by Sam and Dan Houser. Famous for the Grand Theft Auto and Red Dead Redemption franchises. Known for massive open-world games with cinematic storytelling.

companies.name: FromSoftware
companies.description: Japanese video game developer founded in 1986. Director Hidetaka Miyazaki led the creation of the Soulslike genre. Known for challenging action RPGs with deep lore, punishing but fair difficulty, and atmospheric world design.

companies.name: Bethesda Game Studios
current_projects: The Elder Scrolls VI - early development
companies.description: American video game developer founded in 1986. Known for massive open-world RPGs including The Elder Scrolls series (Morrowind, Oblivion, Skyrim) and the Fallout series (from Fallout 3 onward). Acquired by Microsoft in 2021.

companies.name: Insomniac Games
companies.description: American video game developer founded in 1994 by Ted Price. Known for the Ratchet & Clank series and the critically acclaimed Marvel's Spider-Man franchise. Acquired by Sony in 2019.

companies.name: Valve Corporation
companies.description: American video game developer, publisher, and digital distribution company founded in 1996 by Gabe Newell and Mike Harrington. Operates Steam, the largest PC gaming platform. Known for pioneering games and hardware like the Steam Deck.

companies.name: Ubisoft
companies.description: French video game company founded in 1986 by the Guillemot family. One of the largest game publishers in the world. Known for open-world action-adventure games, particularly the Assassin's Creed franchise.
